raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":1.0,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.0,"R1.6":0.3,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.1":"The section leads with a gap statement and a specific contribution claim (4.2 NDCG@5 gain, new SOTA); a review should verify that this framing is coherent and that the stated contribution is actually demonstrated by the artifact.","R1.2":"The concluding sentence infers that both the contrastive objective and the IG formulation are 'essential ingredients' based solely on ablation deltas; checking whether that inferential leap is logically warranted is a core review task.","R1.3":"Load-bearing: paragraph one explicitly claims improvement 'over prior work', yet the experiment block compares only two internal ablations (ClarifyRank-Lex, ClarifyRank-NoIG) with no external baseline. The evidence does not support the stated claim, making this the most critical dimension in the section.","R1.4":"The SOTA assertion depends entirely on the prior-work comparison that the experiments do not instantiate; assessing whether the novelty claim is real and properly contextualized against external systems is central to any technical review."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should this review focus primarily on scientific/technical accuracy (logical validity of claims, evidence-claim alignment, novelty support against external baselines) or on writing quality (prose clarity, sentence structure, style)?"}

rationale.R1.1: The section leads with a gap statement and a specific contribution claim (4.2 NDCG@5 gain, new SOTA); a review should verify that this framing is coherent and that the stated contribution is actually demonstrated by the artifact.
rationale.R1.2: The concluding sentence infers that both the contrastive objective and the IG formulation are 'essential ingredients' based solely on ablation deltas; checking whether that inferential leap is logically warranted is a core review task.
rationale.R1.3: Load-bearing: paragraph one explicitly claims improvement 'over prior work', yet the experiment block compares only two internal ablations (ClarifyRank-Lex, ClarifyRank-NoIG) with no external baseline. The evidence does not support the stated claim, making this the most critical dimension in the section.
rationale.R1.4: The SOTA assertion depends entirely on the prior-work comparison that the experiments do not instantiate; assessing whether the novelty claim is real and properly contextualized against external systems is central to any technical review.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this review focus primarily on scientific/technical accuracy (logical validity of claims, evidence-claim alignment, novelty support against external baselines) or on writing quality (prose clarity, sentence structure, style)?
